text:
PET-CT SCAN WITH 18F-FDG
Whole Body with Dedicated Head and Neck Acquisition

INDICATION:
52-year-old woman with newly diagnosed left base of tongue squamous cell carcinoma, p16 positive (HPV-related). Referred for whole-body metabolic staging prior to initiation of definitive therapy.

TECHNIQUE:
Intravenous 18F-fluorodeoxyglucose was administered following a minimum 6-hour fast (serum glucose at time of injection: 88 mg/dL). Standard uptake period of 60 minutes. Whole-body PET acquisition from vertex to mid-thigh performed in conjunction with low-dose CT for attenuation correction and anatomic localization. Dedicated head and neck CT was acquired with IV contrast (Omnipaque 350, 100 mL). Patient was positioned supine with arms at sides. No significant patient motion artifact.

FINDINGS:

HEAD AND NECK:
PRIMARY SITE: There is a focal area of markedly elevated FDG uptake within the left base of tongue, corresponding to the known primary malignancy. The lesion demonstrates avid, heterogeneous tracer accumulation with an SUVmax of 12.4. On the co-registered contrast-enhanced CT, this corresponds to an ill-defined area of asymmetric mucosal thickening and soft tissue fullness of the left base of tongue measuring approximately 2.1 x 1.8 cm. The mass does not extend to the vallecula or pre-epiglottic fat plane. There is no radiographic evidence of involvement of the midline tongue base or right hemibase of tongue. No osseous involvement of the hyoid bone or adjacent mandible.

REGIONAL LYMPH NODES: A single hypermetabolic left level IIA cervical lymph node is identified, measuring 2.1 cm in short-axis diameter on CT, with an SUVmax of 9.8. The node demonstrates loss of normal fatty hilum and mild internal heterogeneity on CT. There is no CT evidence of extranodal extension (no perinodal soft tissue stranding or capsular irregularity). No additional pathologically enlarged or hypermetabolic lymph nodes are identified in the ipsilateral neck at levels IB, III, IV, V, or in the retropharyngeal or parapharyngeal regions. The contralateral right neck lymph nodes appear within normal limits in size and show no abnormal FDG uptake. No hypermetabolic supraclavicular or mediastinal adenopathy.

OROPHARYNX AND HYPOPHARYNX: The remainder of the oropharyngeal mucosa appears unremarkable without additional sites of focal FDG uptake or suspicious mucosal abnormality. The soft palate, tonsillar fossae bilaterally, posterior pharyngeal wall, and hypopharynx are without discrete hypermetabolic lesions. No evidence of a second primary mucosal malignancy.

SALIVARY GLANDS: Bilateral parotid glands appear symmetric and without focal mass or abnormal tracer uptake. Submandibular glands are unremarkable.

THYROID: Homogeneous appearance without focal uptake. No dominant nodule.

SKULL BASE AND ORBITS: No osseous destructive lesion or abnormal marrow FDG uptake to suggest skull base involvement. Orbits appear unremarkable.

CHEST:
LUNGS: The lungs are clear. No pulmonary nodules, masses, or consolidations. No pleural effusion. No hypermetabolic pulmonary lesions.

MEDIASTINUM: No pathologically enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes. No FDG-avid mediastinal disease. The mediastinal structures including the heart, great vessels, and esophagus appear unremarkable.

ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:
LIVER: Homogeneous hepatic parenchyma without focal lesion or abnormal uptake. Normal hepatic background FDG activity.

SPLEEN: Within normal limits in size and tracer distribution.

PANCREAS, ADRENAL GLANDS, KIDNEYS: No focal hypermetabolic lesion identified. Bilateral kidneys demonstrate physiologic tracer excretion. No adrenal nodule with abnormal uptake.

LYMPH NODES (ABDOMEN/PELVIS): No retroperitoneal, mesenteric, or pelvic lymphadenopathy with abnormal FDG avidity.

BONE:
No focal areas of abnormal osseous FDG uptake to suggest skeletal metastatic involvement. No lytic or sclerotic osseous lesion on the low-dose attenuation correction CT.

OTHER:
No soft tissue masses or FDG-avid lesions identified elsewhere in the imaged field. Physiologic bowel and urinary bladder activity noted.

IMPRESSION:
1. Intensely hypermetabolic left base of tongue mass (SUVmax 12.4), consistent with the patient's known primary oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma. The lesion appears confined to the left hemibase of tongue without evidence of deep infiltration, skull base involvement, or extension across the midline.

2. Single hypermetabolic left level IIA cervical lymph node (SUVmax 9.8, 2.1 cm short axis), representing the known ipsilateral regional nodal metastasis. No contralateral nodal involvement. No bilateral or multilevel cervical nodal disease identified.

3. No evidence of distant metastatic disease. The thorax, abdomen, pelvis, and osseous structures are without FDG-avid abnormality.

4. No synchronous primary malignancy identified in the pharynx, larynx, esophagus, or elsewhere in the surveyed field.

Overall, findings are consistent with locoregional disease only. No distant spread identified on this examination.

Radiologist: [Electronically signed]
Attending Radiologist, Division of Nuclear Medicine/PET Imaging
